content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

April 18, 1985


Lois Shade
811 East Juanita Avenue
Glendora, CA 91740

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-85-066

Dear Ms. Shade:

Thank you for your letter requesting advice concerning your
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

You are a member of the Glendora City Council and you own
residential property adjacent to the Sunflower School, a school
which the Charter Oak Unified School District has scheduled for
closure. Is it a conflict of interest for you to serve as a
member of the advisory committee on school site utilization
which will make recommendations to the governing board of the
Charter Oak Unified School District about site utilization for
schools that are being closed?

CONCLUSION

The Political Reform Act does not prohibit you from serving
as a member of the advisory committee on school site
utilization.

DISCUSSION

Pursuant to Education Code Section 39296, the governing
board of a school district may, and in certain cases is required
_____________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

Lois Shade
April 18, 1985
Page 2


to, establish an advisory committee on school site utilization
to advise the governing board in the development of
district-wide policies and procedures governing the use or
disposition of school buildings or space in school buildings
which is not needed for school purposes. The advisory committee
is required to, among other things, determine the amount of
surplus space in the district, establish a priority list for use
of surplus space that will be acceptable to the community,
provide for hearings of community input to the committee on
acceptable uses of space, and forward to the district governing
board a report recommending uses of surplus space. Education
Code Section 39298. The governing board of the school district
must make the final decision on the use of surplus real property
of the school district. Education Code Section 39360. The
provisions regarding the establishment of an advisory committee
on school site utilization will be repealed December 31, 1986.
Education Code Section 39299.5.

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in, or attempting to use his official position to
influence any governmental decision in which he knows or has
reason to know, he has a financial interest. Section 87100 does
not apply to members of a board or commission which is purely
advisory and possess no decisionmaking authority, such as the
advisory committee on school site utilization. 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18700(a)(1).

Although Section 87100 does not apply to members of the
advisory committee on school site utilization, that section does
apply to any member of a city council. Because you are a member
of the Glendora City Council, Section 87100 requires you to
refrain from making, participating in, or attempting to use your
official position to influence any governmental decision in
which you have a financial interest. There is currently no
decision pending before the Glendora City Council which would
affect the use of the Sunflower School property. However, your
question requires a discussion of the prohibition on using your
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
you have a financial interest when that decision is before
another agency.

Commission regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700
currently provides that a public official attempts to use his
official position to influence a governmental decision if the
official furthers, or attempts to affect in any manner, any
decision within or before his own agency, or any decision before
any agency which is appointed by or is subject to the budgetary
control of his agency. 2 Cal. Adm.Code Section 18700(e). A

Lois Shade
April 18, 1985
Page 3


public official is not prohibited from appearing before an
agency as a member of the general public to represent himself on
matters related solely to his personal interest.  2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18700(f)(1).

     The advisory committee on school site utilization and the
governing board of the Charter Oak Unified School District are
independent of the appointive or budgetary control of the
Glendora City Council; therefore, current law does not restrict
your actions as a member of the advisory committee on school
site utilization due to your position as a Councilmember for the
City of Glendora.

     The current provisions of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700
may be amended by the Commission.  I have enclosed a copy of the
Notice of Proposed Changes and the text of the proposed
regulation, which is scheduled to be heard by the Commission on
May 7, 1985.  If the regulation is amended and you wish to
request additional advice, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                Very truly yours,

                                [signature]
                                Kathryn E. Donovan
                                Counsel
                                Legal Division

KED:Plh
Enclosures

Bankara Milman
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Milman:

[illegible] 17 3 12 AM 85

March 9, 1985

Please give written advice as per Section 83114 (B) on the following:

On March 5, 1985, I received a letter addressed to Ms. Lois Shade
from the Charter Oak Unified School District requesting that
I sit with the committee to study site utilization for two
schools that are being closed in our area. The enclosed map
indicates that relationship of one of the schools to home.
This issue is further complicated by the fact that I am a council
member with the City of Glendora. The second school site to be
closed is a considerable distance (approximately 10 miles) away
and within the jurisdiction of the City of Covina. One other
member of the committee, Ms. Roy Maddock and myself have sat down
with each one of the school board members and discussed school re-
organization and its effects on the community and their reasons
for the closure. Another committee member is my neighbor, directly
to the east and adjacent also to the Sunflower school site.

The concerns I have and those of my constituents in this case
relate to the land use issues and the fact that the school dis-
trict, to this point, has not been in touch with the City of
Glendora concerning the appropriate use of this facility in this
area. You will see the material that was given to the school
board by myself and Ms. Maddock.

Because of the sensitivity of this issue I am concerned about a
potential conflict of interest if I sit with this committee either
as a citizen on city council member because of past actions and
because of location of property and that of my neighbor and the
fact that I am a council member.

As I understand it, this committee is formed and will function
under guidelines set within the education code to deal with school
closures. Other members of the committee include:

Betty Banks          Supervisor, Charter Oak Unified Schools
Joe DeCarolis       teacher within the District
Craig Jennings      Planning Director for City of Covina
Bob Frick          Asst. Superintendent of Business Services for
                   Charter Oak Unified School District
Roy Maddock        Homeowner and Parent and Former employee of Glendora
Eleanor Miller     Homeowner, Parent, and Personnel Commissioner
Eleanor Rich       Citizen, Homeowner adjacent to one of the schools
                   to be closed
Shirley Shaffer    Retired educator
Wayne Thomas       Parent, homeowner and employed by General Telephone
                   Company

Any help and quick response would be much appreciated. Additional
information may be obtained by calling me at my home. Usually
early morning is best.

Sincerely,

Lois Shade
811 E. Juanita Avenue
Glendora, California 91740
818-963-3895

State of California

Memorandum

To      :   File No. A-85-066                          Date  :  July 15, 1985


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Kathy Donovan  [illegible]

Subject :  Deadline Extension


         On April 8, 1985, I spoke with Lois Shade about her request
for advice.  I informed her that, since the school site
utilization committee is purely advisory, her participation as a
citizen member of that committee did not present a conflict of
interest situation.  However, I informed her that in her capacity
as a member of the City Council, she could have a conflict of
interest if the City Council decides to take any action on the
subject.   However, she would be able to appear before the City
Council to represent her own personal interests.  I told her the
letter would be finished soon, but probably not by April 12.  She
indicated that was not a problem for her.

sections.question: You are a member of the Glendora City Council and you own residential property adjacent to the Sunflower School, a school which the Charter Oak Unified School District has scheduled for closure. Is it a conflict of interest for you to serve as a member of the advisory committee on school site utilization which will make recommendations to the governing board of the Charter Oak Unified School District about site utilization for schools that are being closed?

sections.analysis:
Pursuant to Education Code Section 39296, the governing board of a school district may, and in certain cases is required

1Lois Shade
April 18, 1985

to, establish an advisory committee on school site utilization to advise the governing board in the development of district-wide policies and procedures governing the use or disposition of school buildings or space in school buildings which is not needed for school purposes. The advisory committee is required to, among other things, determine the amount of surplus space in the district, establish a priority list for use of surplus space that will be acceptable to the community, provide for hearings of community input to the committee on acceptable uses of space, and forward to the district governing board a report recommending uses of surplus space. Education Code Section 39298. The governing board of the school district must make the final decision on the use of surplus real property of the school district. Education Code Section 39360. The provisions regarding the establishment of an advisory committee on school site utilization will be repealed December 31, 1986. Education Code Section 39299.5.

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in, or attempting to use his official position to influence any governmental decision in which he knows, or has reason to know, he has a financial interest. Section 87100 does not apply to members of a board or commission which is purely advisory and possesses no decisionmaking authority, such as the advisory committee on school site utilization. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(1).

Although Section 87100 does not apply to members of the advisory committee on school site utilization, that section does apply to any member of a city council. Because you are a member of the Glendora City Council, Section 87100 requires you to refrain from making, participating in, or attempting to use your official position to influence any governmental decision in which you have a financial interest. There is currently no decision pending before the Glendora City Council which would affect the use of the Sunflower School property. However, your question requires a discussion of the prohibition on using your official position to influence a governmental decision in which you have a financial interest when that decision is before another agency.

Commission regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 currently provides that a public official attempts to use his official position to influence a governmental decision if the official furthers, or attempts to affect in any manner, any decision within or before his own agency, or any decision before any agency which is appointed by or is subject to the budgetary control of his agency. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(e).

The letter discusses the legal status of a public official appearing before an agency as a member of the general public to represent himself on matters related solely to his personal interest, citing 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(f)(1). It states that the advisory committee on school site utilization and the governing board of the Charter Oak Unified School District are independent of the appointive or budgetary control of the Glendora City Council; therefore, current law does not restrict your actions as a member of the advisory committee on school site utilization due to your position as a Councilmember for the City of Glendora.

The letter also mentions that the current provisions of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 may be amended by the Commission. It encloses a copy of the Notice of Proposed Changes and the text of the proposed regulation, which is scheduled to be heard by the Commission on May 7, 1985. If the regulation is amended and you wish to request additional advice, please contact Kathryn E. Donovan at (916) 322-5901.
